reachability_artifacts: manual-smoke-step: STRICT-binary sworn board --release 2026-06-16-critical-journey-resolutions on the live fired repo (~/projects/fired, clean before+after — Rule 11) renders the full board with 0 'cannot unmarshal object' errors; S05-engine-owned-real-dollars (4 object-form open_deferrals) reads 'verified [verifier]' (reachability/ac08-reachability.txt)

manual-smoke-step: direct state.Read on real fired S05 status.json — all 4 deferrals read OK; acknowledged_by + acknowledged_at populate the NEW named fields, acknowledgement='' (real pre-migration data, confirms AC-11), id/field preserved in Extra (reachability/ac08-direct-read.txt). The fired LOOP was NOT run on the strict binary (AC-11 sequencing: gated on #40).

design_decisions.choice: Carrier representation for open_deferrals/violations: structs with named schema fields plus an Extra overflow map and custom (Un)MarshalJSON, so unknown coach keys (id/description/acknowledged_by) round-trip without loss.
options: struct + Extra overflow map + custom marshalers (chosen): typed access for the Rule-10 consumer, preserves unknown keys, deterministic map-based marshal
type Deferral map[string]any: loss-free but no typed fields; AC-03 requires structs
[]json.RawMessage: byte-perfect round-trip but zero typed access
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Coach (Brad), 2026-07-01: Deferral/Violation as structs with named schema fields plus an Extra map[string]json.RawMessage overflow and custom (Un)MarshalJSON. Chosen over map[string]any and []json.RawMessage because AC-03 requires typed structs that preserve unknown keys and the Rule-10 boundary consumer needs typed Item/Why access; map-based MarshalJSON yields deterministic byte-stable output (drift-gate safe).
design_decisions.rationale: Keystone prerequisite (baton.ValidateSchema) touching the on-disk status.json contract across ~15 files; the planner ratified the direction (structs + preserve-unknowns) in spec rationale + AC-03, and the Coach ratified the representation mechanism + determinism guarantee at design review.

design_decisions.choice: open_deferrals canonical shape defined STRICTLY and ADDITIVELY (required [why, tracking, acknowledgement, acknowledged_by], acknowledged_at optional; NO anyOf), with real coach data migrated to add acknowledgement rather than the schema loosened to accept acknowledged_by as a substitute.
options: strict additive + migrate data (chosen): canonical contract stays strict, vendored schema matches upstream baton with no drift, fails loud on unmigrated artefacts; cost = a one-time 127-deferral cutover migration (#40)

anyOf acknowledgement|acknowledged_by (first cut, REVERSED): no data migration needed, but bakes permanent drift into the vendored schema and conflates acknowledged_by (who) with acknowledgement (Rule-2 plain-text told-evidence)
read-only tolerance, no schema change: relocates the failure from read to first write-back; rejected in session 1
design_decisions.human_decision: Reversed by Coach (Brad), 2026-07-01 (replan 61df7ac, spec Pin-1-REVISED amendment): replace the first cut's anyOf with the strict additive required-set. A name (acknowledged_by) is not Rule 2's plain-text 'told' evidence (acknowledgement); since all artefacts are operator-owned (no wild data) the canonical shape is defined strictly and the data migrated (AC-11 / #40), and the shape pushed up to baton (#38). Strict is also more fail-closed.
design_decisions.rationale: Defines the on-disk + upstream canonical deferral contract; hard to reverse once vendored and PR'd up to baton. Implemented as schema required-set + named Go fields; the carrier mechanism (decision[0]) is unchanged.

design_decisions.choice: Display-only consumers read through Status.DeferralStrings()/Verification.ViolationStrings() projection helpers rather than each call site being rewritten; oracle SliceState.Violations stays []string.
design_decisions.rationale: Bounds the migration diff to the contract surface (Rule 6 scope ceiling); reversible and local.

design_decisions.choice: Rule-10 CheckBoundaryMocks/isDeclared match declared mocks against the Deferral's description-bearing fields (Item + Why) only, not Tracking/Acknowledgement.
design_decisions.rationale: Tracking/Acknowledgement are IDs/URLs that could spuriously contain a boundary keyword and over-declare; matching Item+Why keeps enforcement at least as strict as the prior free-form string match (AC-05).

design_decisions.choice: verdict->state bridge (run/slice.go) wraps verdict.Result.Violations ([]string) into []Violation via Violation{Description: s}; verdict layer stays []string.
design_decisions.rationale: sworn-generated data (not coach-read), so no field-loss concern; ViolationStrings() reproduces the same display string for the oracle.

effort_complexity.rationale: Careful atomic type migration touching the Rule-10 no-mock-boundary, with a round-trip-fidelity trap (fired data uses acknowledged_by, schema requires acknowledgement); ~15 files.
